<!--
Thanks for contributing to the FalseWork Papers.

Please briefly describe your contribution below, following the type of PR
you're submitting. See CONTRIBUTING.md for full acceptance criteria.
-->

## Type

<!-- Check one. -->

- [ ] Validation (verifying a stated claim)
- [ ] Correction (fixing an error)
- [ ] Formalization (Lean 4 or equivalent)
- [ ] Translation
- [ ] Outreach / documentation
- [ ] Tooling / infrastructure
- [ ] Other: ______

## Summary

<!-- One or two sentences: what does this PR change, and why? -->

## Related issue(s)

<!-- Link to any related issues. Use "Fixes #N" to auto-close on merge. -->

## For validations and corrections

**Paper and version:** <!-- e.g., Paper 3 v9.1 -->
**Section:** <!-- e.g., § 4 -->
**Impact on version:** <!-- none / minor patch (no version bump) / bump to [vX.Y] -->

## For formalizations

**Paper claim formalized:** <!-- which claim, which paper section -->
**mathlib4 version tested against:** <!-- e.g., 2026-04-15 release -->
**`lake build` result:** <!-- passing / partially passing with documented axioms / failing -->

## Attribution

<!--
If you'd like to be credited publicly, give us the name to use and briefly
describe your relevant background (one sentence). If you prefer to remain
anonymous or pseudonymous, say so. Anonymous contributions are accepted;
validations carrying epistemic weight benefit from a verifiable identity.
-->

## Checklist

- [ ] I have read [`CONTRIBUTING.md`](../CONTRIBUTING.md)
- [ ] My contribution matches the acceptance criteria for its type
- [ ] I have linked the relevant issue(s)
- [ ] For content changes to a paper: the paper's internal version stamp and revision note have been updated (or, if not, I have explained why in the PR description)
- [ ] I am submitting this under the project's license (CC-BY-4.0 for papers, Apache-2.0 for code)
